methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
IQM Quantum Computers is a Series B-stage quantum computing company that has raised $600M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 publicly traded peers (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and others operating in quantum computing hardware and software.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, using the $600M in total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement. This produces an implied valuation of $1.1B. Peer-median triangulation was not viable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables within quantum computing; while 7 same-sector peers exist, the cohort lacks the requisite 3+ datapoints in matched valuation tier and stage bands necessary for reliable median extraction without introducing sector-wide noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $969M (low) to $1.3B (high), centered on $1.1B. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x multiplier given quantum computing's capital intensity and the absence of a mature peer-derived benchmark. The single-method result reflects genuine scarcity of direct comparables at IQM's stage and market cap level, and any refined valuation would benefit from disclosed financial performance metrics (revenue, burn rate, customer concentration) currently unavailable in the dataset.
